content:
# Acts Chapter 18 -- The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus, PDF pages 247-248 | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/ACT_018_page_001.jpg, ACT_018_page_002.jpg

---

**1** After these-[things], having-departed from Athens, he-came to Corinth.

**2** And having-found a certain Jew [by] name Aquila, of-Pontus by-race, recently having-come from Italy, and Priscilla his wife, because Claudius had-commanded all the Jews to-depart from Rome, he-came-to them,

**3** and because of-being of-the-same-trade he-remained with them, and they-were-working, for they-were tent-makers by-trade.

**4** And he-was-reasoning in the synagogue every sabbath, and was-persuading Jews and Greeks.

**5** And when both Silas and Timothy came-down from Macedonia, Paul was-being-constrained by-the word, testifying to-the Jews [that] Jesus [is] the Christ.

**6** And [when] they were-opposing and blaspheming, having-shaken-out [his] garments, he-said to them, "Your blood [be] upon your head; I [am] clean; from now I-will-go to the Gentiles."

**7** And having-moved from-there, he-went into [the] house of a certain [man] named Titius Justus, [a] worshiper of-God, whose house was adjoining the synagogue.

**8** And Crispus, the ruler-of-the-synagogue, believed in-the Lord with his whole household, and many of-the Corinthians hearing were-believing and were-being-baptized.

**9** And the Lord said through a vision in [the] night to-Paul, "Do-not be-afraid, but speak and do-not be-silent,

**10** because I am with you, and no-one will-set-upon you to-harm you, because there-is a people to-me much in this city."

**11** And he-sat a year and six months, teaching among them the word of-God.

**12** And [when] Gallio was-being proconsul of-Achaia, the Jews with-one-accord rose-up-against Paul and brought him before the judgment-seat,

**13** saying that "Contrary-to the law this-[one] is-persuading men to-worship God."

**14** And [when] Paul was-about to-open [his] mouth, Gallio said to the Jews, "If indeed it-were some wrongdoing or wicked villainy, O Jews, according-to reason I-would-have-borne-with you;

**15** but if they-are questions about a word and names and the law according-to you, you-will-see-to-[it] yourselves; a judge of these-[things] I do-not wish to-be."

**16** And he-drove them from the judgment-seat.

**17** And they all, having-seized Sosthenes the ruler-of-the-synagogue, were-beating [him] before the judgment-seat; and none of these-[things] was-a-concern to-Gallio.

**18** And Paul, having-remained yet many days, having-bid-farewell to-the brothers, was-sailing-away to Syria, and with him Priscilla and Aquila, having-shorn [his] head in Cenchreae, for he-had a vow.

**19** And they-arrived at Ephesus, and he-left those-[ones] there; but he-himself, having-entered into the synagogue, reasoned with-the Jews.

**20** And [when] they were-asking [him] to-remain for a longer time, he-did-not consent,

**21** but having-bid-farewell and having-said, "I-will-return again to you, God willing," he-set-sail from Ephesus.

**22** And having-gone-down to Caesarea, having-gone-up and having-greeted the church, he-went-down to Antioch.

**23** And having-spent some time, he-went-out, passing-through in-order the Galatian region and Phrygia, strengthening all the disciples.

**24** And a certain Jew, Apollos [by] name, an Alexandrian by-race, a learned man, arrived at Ephesus, being powerful in the Scriptures.

**25** This-[one] was having-been-instructed [in] the way of-the Lord, and being-fervent in-the spirit, he-was-speaking and teaching accurately the-[things] concerning Jesus, knowing only the baptism of-John.

**26** And this-[one] began to-speak-boldly in the synagogue. And having-heard him, Priscilla and Aquila took-him-aside and more-accurately expounded to-him the way of-God.

**27** And [when] he was-wishing to-pass-through into Achaia, the brothers having-encouraged [him] wrote to-the disciples to-receive him; who, having-arrived, contributed much to-the-[ones] having-believed through grace.

**28** For powerfully he-was-thoroughly-refuting the Jews publicly, showing through the Scriptures [that] Jesus is the Christ.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

**Acts 18 -- Codex Sinaiticus Observations**

1. **Verse 2 -- "Priscilla"**: Sinaiticus reads ΠΡΙΣΚΙΛΛΑΝ (Priscillan/Priscilla) here. Some later manuscripts read "Prisca" (the shortened form Paul uses in his epistles). The Sinaiticus consistently uses the diminutive "Priscilla" throughout Acts.

2. **Verse 5 -- "constrained by the word"**: Sinaiticus reads ΣΥΝΕΙΧΕΤΟ ΤΩ ΛΟΓΩ ("was-being-constrained by-the word"). The Byzantine tradition reads ΤΩ ΠΝΕΥΜΑΤΙ ("by-the spirit"). This is a significant variant: Sinaiticus portrays Paul as pressed by the urgency of the message itself rather than by the Spirit.

3. **Verse 7 -- "Titius Justus"**: Sinaiticus reads ΤΙΤΙΟΥ ΙΟΥΣΤΟΥ. Some manuscripts read "Titus Justus"; others omit "Titius" entirely, reading simply "Justus." Codex Bezae (D) reads "Titus Justus."

4. **Verse 17 -- "they all"**: Sinaiticus reads ΠΑΝΤΕΣ ("all") without specifying who seized Sosthenes. The Western text (D) adds "the Greeks" (οι Ελληνες), while some Byzantine manuscripts read "all the Greeks." The ambiguity in Sinaiticus leaves open whether it was the Jewish crowd or the Greek bystanders who beat the synagogue ruler.

5. **Verse 21 -- Omission**: Sinaiticus omits the phrase "I must by all means keep the coming feast in Jerusalem" found in the Textus Receptus and some later manuscripts. The shorter reading (also in P74, Alexandrinus, Vaticanus) is considered more original by most text critics.

6. **Verse 25 -- "having-been-instructed"**: The Greek ΗΝ ΚΑΤΗΧΗΜΕΝΟΣ (pluperfect periphrastic) indicates Apollos had been catechized at some prior point -- his instruction was a completed past event, not an ongoing process.

7. **Verse 26 -- "Priscilla and Aquila"**: Sinaiticus places Priscilla (ΠΡΙΣΚΙΛΛΑ) before Aquila here, matching the order in several key witnesses. This reversed order (wife before husband) may indicate her prominence in the early church's teaching ministry.

8. **Verse 27 -- "through grace"**: The phrase ΔΙΑ ΤΗΣ ΧΑΡΙΤΟΣ ("through grace") could modify either "having-believed" (those who believed through grace) or "contributed" (he contributed through grace). The Greek word order in Sinaiticus favors attaching it to "having-believed," meaning the Achaian believers had come to faith through [God's] grace.

-- EMET
